---
title: "WINK Lab"
subtitle: "Wireless Intelligence for Networks and Knowledge"
image: "files/icon/icon_wink.png"

about:
    id: lab-info
    template: jolla
    image-width: 10em
    image-shape: rectangle
listing:
    - id: phd
      contents:
        - people/phd.yml
      type: grid
      grid-columns: 4
      fields: [title, description, date, image]
      max-description-length: 200
      sort: 
        - "date desc"
      page-size: 100
      categories: false
      sort-ui: false
      filter-ui: false
    - id: undergrad
      contents:
        - people/undergrad.yml
      type: grid
      grid-columns: 4
      fields: [title, description, date, image]
      max-description-length: 200
      sort: 
        - "date desc"
      page-size: 100
      categories: false
      sort-ui: false
      filter-ui: false
    - id: equipment
      contents:
        - equipment/*.yml
      type: grid
      grid-columns: 3
      fields: [title, description, image, categories]
      max-description-length: 200
      sort: 
        - "date desc"
      page-size: 100
      categories: false
      sort-ui: false
      filter-ui: true
date-format: "MMM, YYYY"
resources: 
    - "people/images/*"
    - "equipment/images/*"
page-layout: full
toc: true
page-navigation: false
title-block-banner: false
---

:::{#lab-info}
:::

The **Wireless Intelligence for Networks and Knowledge (WINK) Lab**, founded by Prof. Kun Qian at the Department of Computer Science, University of Virginia, aims to develop wireless technologies that enable ambient intelligence and ubiquitous connectivity with ensured interpretability, reliability, and security. Our current research focuses on the following areas:

- AI for wireless sensing and communication, e.g., generative models
- Wireless IoT/mobile system and hardware, e.g., backscatter and RIS
- Applications of wireless sensing, e.g., health, transportation, environment

## Lab Members

### Ph.D. Students
:::{#phd}
:::

<!-- ### Master's Students -->

### Undergraduate Students
:::{#undergrad}
:::

### Alumni

WINK Lab is young and has no alumni yet.

## Lab Equipment

WINK Lab possesses a variety of wireless communication and sensing equipment. We are open to collaborations and welcome visitors to our lab. If you are interested in our research or have any questions, please feel free to contact [Prof. Kun Qian](mailto:kqian@virginia.edu).
